'''Harvey Makin''' (born 17 November 2003) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]], on a short-term loan from the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in [[RFL League One]] and the [[Barrow Raiders]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. Makin has also played on loan at the [[London Broncos]] in both the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Super League]], as well the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League|top flight]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/harvey-makin-58769.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

==Background==
Makin was born in [[Wigan, Greater Manchester]], England.<ref>[https://www.theoldhamtimes.co.uk/sport/25667519.oldham-bolster-pack-two-new-signings/ Oldham bolster pack with two new signings]</ref>

He played for [[Wigan St Judes]] as a junior.<ref>[https://www.salfordreddevils.com/harvey-makin-joins-salford-red-devils-on-loan/ HARVEY MAKIN JOINS SALFORD RED DEVILS ON LOAN]</ref> He played for [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in the [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Origin series]].<ref>[https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/wigan-warriors-prop-makes-short-term-super-league-loan-move-with-immediate-effect-5084052 Wigan Warriors prop makes short-term Super League loan move with immediate effect]</ref>

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
Makin made his professional debut in June 2023 on loan at the [[London Broncos]] against the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[2025 RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>[https://www.barrowrlfc.com/latest-news/raiders-bring-in-harvey-makin-on-loan/ Raiders bring in Harvey Makin on loan]</ref>

Makin spent the first half of 2024 on a second loan spell at the London Broncos where he made his Super League debut. He was recalled half way through the season and sent on loan again to the [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-rising-prop-harvey-makin-heads-back-to-championship-on-loan-4709673</ref>

In July 2025 he made his [[Wigan Warriors]] debut in the [[Super League]] against the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>[https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-youngster-relishing-championship-loan-with-ultimate-goal-clear-to-see-5440153 Wigan Warriors youngster relishing Championship loan with ultimate goal clear to see]</ref>

===Oldham RLFC (loan)===
On 10 March 2026 he returned to parent club [[Wigan Warriors]] from his season-long loan at [[Oldham RLFC]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Mar 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Young Wigan Warriors forward returns from season-long Championship loan as potential hailed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/harvey-makin-recalled-from-oldham-loan-10-03-2026}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders (loan)===
On 20 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Mar 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Wigan Warriors forward Harvey Makin lands fresh Championship loan as destination confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/harvey-makin-barrow-loan-20-03-2026}}</ref>
